source: /Users/jerod/refs/nd/opin/pdfs/2022/2022ND27.pdf pages 3-4 (PDF line 192: page number '4' inserted mid-line between columns)
evidence: PDF source shows page number '4' on its own line (line 192) between 'under the' (end of page 3) and 'influence of alcohol' (beginning of page 4). During text extraction and ingestion, this page number was incorrectly merged into the running text as 'under the 4 influence'. The correct text per the printed opinion is 'under the influence of alcohol'. Confirmed in PDF by visual inspection: paragraph begins at line 188 'follow after a person has tested over the legal limit for driving under the', continues at line 192 with isolated page number '4', then resumes at line 193 'influence of alcohol'.

old_exact: do not apply (citing N.D.R.Ev. 1101(d)(3)(C)). to preliminary hearings in criminal cases. Id.
new_exact: do not apply to preliminary hearings in criminal cases. Id. (citing N.D.R.Ev. 1101(d)(3)(C)).
note: Citation parenthetical misplaced within sentence in DB. The parenthetical '(citing N.D.R.Ev. 1101(d)(3)(C)).' appears mid-sentence after 'do not apply' in the DB, creating a sentence fragment 'to preliminary hearings...' Following the authoritative PDF, the citation should follow the complete dependent clause 'to preliminary hearings in criminal cases' and cite only to the rule via the parenthetical after 'Id.'

source: PDF pdftotext extraction; ¶31 (¶31] When we are faced with conflicting rules... Statutes and rules are to be construed in a way which does not render them useless...
evidence: The DB text contains 'Srender' (one occurrence at line 499), which is an OCR misread of the letter 'S' being prepended to 'render'. The authoritative PDF source clearly shows 'render' in the same position. This is a straightforward OCR character error where a stray mark or ligature was misread as the letter 'S'.
note: Image-verified via mutool render of PDF page 12 showing 'Statutes and rules are to be construed in a way which does not render them useless' with clear 'render' (not 'Srender')

evidence:
PDF shows section heading 'III' appearing BEFORE paragraph 13 (with blank lines in between), while DB has 'III' appearing AFTER paragraph 13. Line-by-line examination confirms PDF structure: '...appeal was untimely.

                                     III

[¶13] Because we are without jurisdiction...' vs DB: '...appeal was untimely.

[¶13] Because we are without jurisdiction...

III'. This is a heading sequence inversion error in the database.

The DB text at [¶12] is missing the authority citation ', N.D.C.C.,' that appears in the authoritative PDF. This is a standard ingestion defect where the statute reference was split and partially lost. The PDF clearly shows 'Section 14-09-06.6(6)(b), N.D.C.C., requires' while the DB only has 'Section 14-09-06.6(6)(b) requires'. Paragraph [¶9] contains the same pattern correctly: 'Section 14-09-06.6(6)(a), N.D.C.C., requires' in both versions, confirming this is the court's standard format.
note: This is a missing text error at the statute/authority level - the N.D.C.C. citation abbreviation that accompanies the statute reference was dropped during ingestion.
